data.title: A Phase II Study of Navelbine (Vinorelbine) In Children With Recurrent Or Refractory Malignancies
data.country: Switzerland, Australia, Canada, New Zealand, Netherlands, United States, Puerto Rico
data.brief_summary:
RATIONALE: Drugs used in chemotherapy use different ways to stop tumor cells from dividing so they stop growing or die.

PURPOSE: Phase II trial to study the effectiveness of vinorelbine in treating children with recurrent or refractory cancer.
data.eligibility_criteria: DISEASE CHARACTERISTICS: Histologically confirmed recurrent or refractory solid malignant tumors of childhood including: Soft tissue sarcoma Rhabdomyosarcoma Nonrhabdomyosarcoma Extraosseous Ewing's sarcoma CNS tumors Astrocytoma Primitive neuroectodermal tumor Atypical teratoid/rhabdoid tumors Epen

data.title: Phase II Study of Vinorelbine + Cyclofosfamide Association Among Patients Reached of Refractory Tumours or in Relapse
data.brief_summary: This is a phase II study to determine the antitumor activity of Vinorelbine and Cyclofosfamide association among patients with refractory tumours or in relapse with rhabdomyosarcomas and other soft tissue tumours, Ewing tumours, osteosarcomas, neuroblastomas or medulloblastomas.
data.eligibility_criteria:
Inclusion Criteria:

* Age \> 12 months and \< 25 years
* Measurable disease
* Score of Lansky \> 30 or World Health Organization (WHO) score \< 2
* Life expectancy \> 2 months
* Satisfactory hematologic conditions:

  * Polynuclear neutrophiles \> 1 X 10\^9/l.
  * Platelets \> 100 X 10\^9/l or \> 5
endpoints: To determine the antitumor activity of Vinorelbine and oral Cyclofosfamide association in refractory tumours or in relapse
data.sponsor: Gustave Roussy, Cancer Campus, Grand Paris

data.title: A Prospective Multicenter Cohort Study of Outcome and Safety Profile of Risk Stratification Oriented Treatment in Pediatric Ewing Sarcoma in China
data.brief_summary: The aim is to evaluate the outcome and safety of risk-stratification oriented treatment for pediatric Ewing sarcoma in multicenters .

data.title: CAMPFIRE: Children's and Young Adult Master Protocol for Innovative Pediatric Research
data.country: Australia, Spain, France, United Kingdom, Belgium, Netherlands, Germany, Italy, United States, Japan
data.brief_summary: The main purpose of the master is to help the research sites and sponsor carry out several clinical trials more efficiently by providing a common research protocol. Individual clinical trials under this master protocol define drug/disease-specific research goals and activities to test them. New studies will be added as new drugs emerge against different cancers. Participation in the trial will depend on how long the benefit lasts.
data.eligibility_criteria:
Inclusion Criteria:

Participants must meet all of the inclusion criteria below. Additional criteria are specified in the protocol amendment (individual addenda) to which the participant will enroll.

* Have either measurable or evaluable disease using standard techniques by the Response Evaluation
endpoints: Number of Participants Allocated to Each ISA
